SEE ALL SERVICES →

Who We Are

Your Neighbors in Pest Control

Founded in 2011 by Tampa native Jason Miller, Horizon Pest Control started with one truck and a mission to protect local families from unwanted pests. What began as a small operation serving South Tampa bungalows has grown into a trusted name across Hillsborough County.

We take a science-based approach to pest management, using the latest techniques adapted for Florida's unique climate. Our team spends time understanding each home's specific needs, whether it's a historic bungalow in Seminole Heights or a new condo along Bayshore Boulevard.

As active members of the Tampa community, we're proud to support local schools and hurricane preparedness initiatives. When you choose Horizon, you're choosing neighbors who care about your family's safety and comfort.

12+ Years
800+ Projects
97% Satisfaction
GET IN TOUCH →

Our Difference

Why Choose Horizon Pest Control

Local Climate Experts

We understand how Tampa's humidity and seasonal rains create ideal conditions for pests, and tailor our approach accordingly.

Historic Home Specialists

Our technicians are trained in treating older Tampa homes without damaging their unique architectural features.

Preventative Focus

We don't just eliminate current pests - we help prevent future infestations through structural recommendations.

Fast Emergency Response

Available 24/7 for urgent situations like wasp nests or rodent sightings during hurricane season.

Ready for a Pest-Free Home?

Call our Tampa team today for a free inspection and customized protection plan.

Get a Free Estimate Call 555-pes-r90

Client Feedback

What Our Clients Say

“

Horizon found termite damage in our South Tampa bungalow that two other inspectors missed. Their detailed report with photos helped us get insurance coverage for the repairs. The technician explained everything clearly and even pointed out vulnerable spots in our historic home's foundation.

Maria C. Termite Inspection
“

After years of battling mosquitoes in our backyard near the Hillsborough River, Horizon's monthly treatments finally let us enjoy our patio again. They noticed our irrigation system was creating standing water we hadn't even seen. Night and day difference - worth every penny.

James T. Mosquito Control
“

We had roof rats getting into our Seminole Heights attic through the palm trees. Horizon sealed all entry points humanely and installed protective guards on our trees. No more scratching noises at night and they followed up twice to make sure the problem was fully resolved.

Lisa & Dan R. Rodent Control
“

As a Tampa Airbnb host, bed bugs were my worst nightmare. Horizon's heat treatment eliminated them completely after guests brought them in. They were discreet, thorough, and helped me implement prevention protocols. Six months later - still bed bug free.

Robert K. Bed Bug Treatment
“

We've used Horizon's quarterly service for our Westchase home for three years. I appreciate how they adjust treatments seasonally - extra attention to ants in spring, palmetto bugs before summer rains. Our technician remembers our house's specific trouble spots each visit.

Sarah J. General Pest Control
“

Found termites in our Brandon home's garage during a renovation. Horizon's localized treatment saved us from tenting the whole house. They took time to explain the different termite species in our area and what signs to watch for in the future.

Carlos M. Termite Treatment

Our Portfolio

Our Recent Work

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

How often should Tampa homes be inspected for termites?
We recommend annual inspections for most homes, but older properties or those with previous termite activity may need biannual checks, especially after our rainy season from June-September.
Do you inspect for drywood termites common in Florida?
Yes, our technicians are trained to identify both subterranean and drywood termite species, including the particularly destructive Formosan termites found along Tampa's coastline.
Will the inspection disrupt my home?
Our non-invasive methods mean minimal disruption - we won't tear into walls or damage finishes. Most inspections take 45-90 minutes depending on your home's size.
